name: XFLR5
description: A free low-order aerodynamic analysis tool for aerofoils, wings, and small aircraft concepts. It is widely used for conceptual wing studies and UAV design.
whyUseIt: XFLR5 gives aerospace learners a fast way to explore lift curves, drag polars, stability, and wing geometry effects without the setup cost of full CFD. It is ideal for early-stage aerodynamic intuition.
fieldPosition: Conceptual and preliminary aerodynamic design, especially for UAVs and light aircraft
engineerWorkflow: Import or define an aerofoil, build the wing or aircraft geometry, generate polars, then compare planform or twist changes before moving to higher-fidelity tools.
managerExpects: Clear evidence that XFLR5 results were used for screening and intuition, not overstated as certification-grade truth.
demoDescription: Compare two wing planforms for the same UAV mission and show how aspect ratio changes induced drag and static margin.

vocabulary.definition: A curve showing aerodynamic behaviour such as lift coefficient versus drag coefficient or angle of attack.
A wing-shape measure comparing span to chord; higher aspect ratio usually reduces induced drag.

beginnerMistakes: Treating low-order output as if it captures viscous or transonic effects perfectly
Comparing concepts without keeping Reynolds number and mission assumptions consistent
